tldr: Dorian Godon won the 3.2 km Villars-sur-Glâne prologue for INEOS Grenadiers, with Ivo Oliveira and Jakob Søderqvist tied for second at +6 seconds. Pogačar — the overall race favourite — opened a distant sixth at +7, deliberately conservative on a technical short course before the climbing days. Full overall GC documentation at depth has not yet stabilised in public coverage at compilation time.

narrative.headline: Godon takes the prologue; Pogačar plays it safe at +7
narrative.openingMoves: 3.2 km ITT, Villars-sur-Glâne city-centre. Technical, power-focused, the standard Romandie opener.
narrative.raceUnfolds: Godon set the reference time; Oliveira and Søderqvist split second on the same gap. Pogačar finished sixth at +7 — Cyclingnews' phrasing: 'overall race favourite Tadej Pogačar finished a distant sixth place, just one second further behind' Oliveira. Deliberately conservative day-one for the team's protected card.
narrative.decision: Prologue established positions to one-digit seconds. The race's GC would be settled on the later climbing stages.
narrative.finale: Overall GC trajectory beyond the prologue is partially documented in current public coverage; this page captures the prologue's reliable picture and notes the favourite hierarchy going into the climbing days.

decisiveMoments.headline: Godon takes the leader's jersey
decisiveMoments.description: Godon — a powerful rouleur-puncheur — set the best reference time, with Oliveira and Søderqvist splitting second at +6. Pogačar deliberately conservative at +7.

teamStories.verdict: Godon prologue win, control of day 1
teamStories.narrative: Day-one stage win + leader's jersey is the clean opening INEOS would have predicted. Godon's TT power matched the technical short course.

UAE's plan was conservative: keep Pogačar in GC range without burning the legs on a 3.2 km technical TT before climbing stages. Mission completed at +7. Oliveira's second on stage gave the team an early secondary podium card.

riderPerformances.name: Tadej Pogačar
riderPerformances.narrative: Sixth in the prologue at +7 — the headline-level GC favourite playing it safe on a technical short course. The week's climbing stages would be the real test; the prologue was a placeholder.

aftermath.headline: Conservative opening
aftermath.body: Romandie's prologue puts the GC contenders within seconds of each other for a week's racing. Godon's leader's jersey will pass to a climber by the weekend; the prologue was about positioning, not outcomes.
